item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I want a tale where 2331 and 2006 vacation in Halloweentown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This scip just made me feel warm inside, something we don't get much of. The ;last interview really nailed in the +1 for me. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is it that I think 2331 should team up with Groover?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something is off with the "ensure that they are still functional" bit but other than that I really liked it. Interesting premise and good use of Foundation field agents. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great.

I especially like the writing for the incident logs, you really get a sense of being in a rave, of seeing and hearing what is being described. The interviews were similarly compelling, and the anomalous light and fog effects were a really neat touch.

+1

item.title: For the record
item.content:encoded:
the image in this SCP is based on:

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Dance_Marathon_at_UF_Rave_Hour.jpg

which is available under Creative Commons share-alike 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: love dit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm upvoting this because I love it, but I'm not a fan of the name being written as "DeadMau5" as opposed to the more widely-used and "Deadmau5" (or the more official stylisation, "deadmau5"). Usually I'd say that different written interpretations of musician's names are personal preference, but I doubt the Foundation wants to come across as a dad attempting to fit in with his own kids; the capitalised M is almost patronising.

(unless it's intended to represent some sort of middle-aged ineptitude of the employee writing the article itself. In which case, that's a nice touch!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a plain off fun SCP. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this hasn't had a new post in a couple months but I just want to point this line out.

It's hard to watch something you've lived for hollow out to a shell of its former self.

really

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really? Even more than "into the fiery orb with ye"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Cash as balls" is my new favorite phrase.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I admit I would totally go to the party of an EDM producer that pretends to be a raving scarecrow with neon blue glowy pumpkin head. That sounds cash as balls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, it's pretty much inevitable that an EDM producer who is a fan of this site will eventually take this as his stage personality. Like DeadMau5 has the dead-mouse-in-his-computer story, Daft Punk has their own little tale, and Scravecrow will have "I read it on a creepypasta site lol."

item.title: Re: AND THEY COMBINE
item.content:encoded:
But, of course, you don't know that you live forever. All you know is that you live longer than the others. Maybe a lot longer, yes, but… as far as you know, you — like the others — only live once.

"Forever" is a LONG time.

item.title: Re: AND THEY COMBINE
item.content:encoded:
cactus scravecrow DJ would be the 682 of partying hard.

What is "YOLO" if you "L" forever?

item.title: Re: AND THEY COMBINE
item.content:encoded:
Cacti live in deserts. They're rarely affected much by autumn weather, and can survive hot, dry summers.

My Broken God, such an entity would quite likely be immortal. We must not let this happens.

item.title: AND THEY COMBINE
item.content:encoded: and they bond together into cactus scravecrow, dj of the ultimate rave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this thing. It might be a little cliche in how it's executed, but I feel like that's part of the charm of the SCP - stereotypical ravers in a stereotypical fashion guided by some odd entity. Some people might gripe about how it's not in strict Foundation style, but that's neutral for me. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: same here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… no scarecrow SCP?
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-872

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the most charming motherfucker we've had in ages here. All my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheerylight. You gotta go with direct opposites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found this pretty "meh" until the last interview, which redeemed the whole article, and made the whole concept good.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
grimwarmness

Grimwhite? Grimgrey? (to contrast with grimdark)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Exactly this. It's the kind of grimwarmness1 that I love; speically because it's rare. Anyway, the result is pretty good. And since Jack is a peace lover, it means you took all the cheap danger-for-upvotes away of it.

Footnotes
1. Is this even a word?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kay, I've edited out most of the dialogue in that vein.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much this. I nearly upvoted upon the revelation that this was apparently the Jack o'Lantern, but a few bits in the logs killed any interest for me, particularly this line here:

Agent Michalsky: "Excuse me, misses, but may you please stop grinding on me I've got some very important busine-"

That just seems overly juvenile.

Overall, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As you wish. I'd also wish that you stop dismissing it as taste, as I actually like the idea, just not how it's performed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a good fucking song, by the way. Nice reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't say I find the idea of a DJ scarecrow terribly interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Grimdark isn't the only way to go, people.

Why is this still repeated like it's a 'thing', like everyone insists upon it? Probably the majority of articles from the past couple years have made no attempt at all at being grimdark. There are anime catgirls, flying fart-cows and -monkeys, and cockgoblins around here now, and three of those four are mainlist articles. Why even say that?

On-topic: I rarely appreciate the re-packaging of old mythological figures, so I'm biased right from the main idea of this. Jack trying to get his old feelgoodz back is alright, and if it was done…tastefully?…enough, I could dig it, but to me this seems like the addition of a pile of cliches. The logs seem to include every character from every party scene from every comedy movie and D.A.R.E. spot made. On the plus side, I choose to believe that I saw the Night at the Roxbury guys in there, and that's slightly cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually instead of arguing pedantic details for another hour in here after an hour of this on the chat, I'm just gonna repeat myself:

I respect your opinion/taste. Thanks for your feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had a delightful mental image of djkaktus replacing the scarecrow

OH GAWDS ME TOO

Just look at him
Kakty

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. I don't know why, It's funny and made me smile. Good work Soulless I'm looking forward to what you come up with next.

Also Ed random sidenote: I had a delightful mental image of djkaktus replacing the scarecrow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god, this is funny and creepy1 and sad at the same time. +1

Agent E-dit: Re-reading it, I can see flaws, but as a kind person my vote stays.

Footnotes
1. I rreeeaaallyy don't like dances

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Scravecrow just wants to relive some kind of feeling from his past. He has absolutely no reason to make it "much more crazier".

"Well, I could tell you plenty about him, but you probably won't believe me. He used to be called Jack, y'know, of the lantern variety. Used to do a lot of work, changing the color of the leaves and making things foggy on dark nights. He was a bit of seasonal worker if y'know what I'm saying."

See this line. Jack, judging by what he did before, could easily use his abilities to enhance the party without hurting anybody. It doesn't need to be violent.

The first log was first contact, as stated in the logs themselves. The second log was maybe 4th contact, where they were much better for it.

Unless there researcher happens to be the very first authority to come across the the SCP, being unprepared is not a wise thing to do, I mean, we are talking about a party that literally materializes and dematerializes, there's got to be rumours or foundation-contact worthy worries on part of the police and other authorities.

I don't actually understand what this means. The researcher wasn't really socially inept for having a few uhs, is she? Jesus, that makes me super socially inept.

Socially inept enough to potentially botch the mission, as given that the approach towards contact was non-violent. You gotta know how to talk your way to the DJ. Not to mention that like the girl said she could just google his name if acquiring second hand info on him was the mission.

As I said before,this was based on things I hear coming out of people's mouths in raves. I've edited it since to tone it down.

It's a rather common stereotype of the clubbing girl. Yes, there's people that talks like this, but they are a minority.

You can have non-premeditated interviews. I didn't have a better word, but if you find one I would love to know.

The best thing I can think of is "audio extract"

No, the line is a basic demographics of the population of people that attend the party. O_o

And those demographics do not go beyond what is expected of raves (regardless of how we feel at the fact that 13 years old do go to raves). So it could be simplified in order to read better.

This isn't necessary because the only purpose was an implication, that's it.

Well, if you make a statement, you sort of have to write down it's cause or hypothesized cause too. I mean, if I'm an O5 and someone gives me a report saying "X thing happens more than normal", I'd be interested in knowing why. Case in context could be thematic (basically showering you with get high messages or facilitating access to drugs) or anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whenever I think I've found a new favorite music SCP, they just keep on coming.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as I know there hasn't been a scarecrow SCP before so… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most of this was addressed in the chat when you had the conversation with me about it. As I said then and I will say now, you are perfectly respected to your sense of taste and if we disagree stylistically that's totally OK.

The anomaly is lackluster - if the creature is supposedly magical in nature, given that it used to do magic stuff before becoming the scravecrow, one would have said a rave with such anomaly would be much more crazier

It says in the last interview that scravecrow wants no trouble, no hurt, no violence. Scravecrow just wants to relive some kind of feeling from his past. He has absolutely no reason to make it "much more crazier".

Researchers and MFTs, which we should remember are specially trained to deal with the SCPs they are assigned to

The first log was first contact, as stated in the logs themselves. The second log was maybe 4th contact, where they were much better for it.

socially inept research

I don't actually understand what this means. The researcher wasn't really socially inept for having a few uhs, is she? Jesus, that makes me super socially inept.

Then there's the whole ditz teenager stereotype on the hands of the girl.

As I said before,this was based on things I hear coming out of people's mouths in raves. I've edited it since to tone it down.

There's also the fact that they are called interviews, but interviews are not that from what I know, those are an event's sound recordings, not a premeditated interview.

You can have non-premeditated interviews. I didn't have a better word, but if you find one I would love to know.

This line treats something that should be assumed of a rave as if it was worthy of mention.

No, the line is a basic demographics of the population of people that attend the party. O_o

This line implies that people drink more alcohol and drugs than normal in a rave, but how much is not pointed out anywhere.

This isn't necessary because the only purpose was an implication, that's it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmmm, didn't like it.

How people acted inside the rave felt too far fetched. From mysteriously protecting the DJ to not reacting to the presence of guns, which could easily start a panic where human stampede effects could happen. Though all in all it was never clarified what guns are these. Handguns could be easily concealed. I recommends further describing the equipment. It could be easily passed off as mental suggestions, but (as of the time of writing, seeing how Soully is still upgrading the skip) article clarifies nothing anomalous happens to the people themselves.

"!SCP-2331-P will generally attract humans typically between 18 to 30 years of age, although humans as young as 13 and as old as 73 have been known to attend."

This line treats something that should be assumed of a rave as if it was worthy of mention. I recommend to simplify it to something along the lines of "attendee age range is what is expected of the typical rave event"

"It is theorized that attendants to SCP-2331-P may be more susceptible to alcohol consumption and drug use upon review of Incident 2331-08, the details of which are still undergoing investigation."

This line implies that people drink more alcohol and drugs than normal in a rave, but how much is not pointed out anywhere. Please clarify. It also leads to the following:

The anomaly is lackluster - if the creature is supposedly magical in nature, given that it used to do magic stuff before becoming the scravecrow, one would have said a rave with such anomaly would be much more crazier, however nothing truly anomalous happens beyond magical disappearance of objects. That feels like wasted potential to me. Worth of mention is the fact that everything dematerializes in front of people, yet nobody reacts. Even for parties where people is commonly high, it's ridiculous to assume none would suspect a thing.

The descriptions of objects and actions are done on an overly formal, verbose manner, which end up being more of a bore than a summary of the events. By writing it like anything regarding the rave is rare or strange, it feels like a soccer mom (with no intention to insult) that has only heard of raves wrote the procedures. I don't think the foundation researchers would be that naive/old fashioned.

The logs themselves feel too cliché. Researchers and MFTs, which we should remember are specially trained to deal with the SCPs they are assigned too, act as if they are completely unprepared to the setting they are in, which treads into the cliché of the socially inept research (It could be another cliché, it's the first one that comes to my mind). Then there's the whole ditz teenager stereotype on the hands of the girl. Dialogue and description of events on the logs felt really ugly. There's also the fact that they are called interviews, but interviews are not that from what I know, those are an event's sound recordings, not a premeditated interview. There's probably a better word, but I can't remember it.

The last log however, I liked.

I think that's all without copypasting the article to my sandbox and making a sentence by sentence analysis. Sorry if I was too rude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn it you did it again. I'm glad it's not super depressing and I love Daft Punk so I loved the team name haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realize this definitely isn't for everyone, but by god do I love it. Grimdark isn't the only way to go, people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddammit it Soulless. You beat me to it with my idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you Leminnes for the name of this thing.

The Song Referenced In The Incident Logs

Thanks to Tagliafierro, Dr_Leonard, SoundChaser, and Paroxysm for the reviews. Thanks to Kate, Kaktus and thekillerax to help maintain my motivation.

For NaNoWriMo.

I'm breaking my palindrome habit for 31.
